=== THE LAW ===
A source enters the corpus ONLY bound to real bytes via provenance.py (manifestation_id + content_hash),
and for task 72's JOURNAL-ONLY policy it must be journal-attributable. A WORKING PAPER / PREPRINT is a
DISCOVERY LEAD, never journal evidence -- peer review changes numbers (Acemoglu's robot effect: 0.37pp in
the NBER WP, 0.2pp in the JPE). Every fetch goes through acquisition.Acquirer; a 429 is THROTTLED ->
BACKEND_FAILED, NEVER "no evidence". `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` must stay GREEN.

WRITE ONLY under /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/ and the shared ledger. Do NOT touch cellcog_composer.py, the main corpus, or
outputs/evidence_cards_bound.json. Another concern: do not clobber outputs/release/.


=== WHAT HAPPENED ===
The acquisition fetch worker was KILLED ~63s into a 55-min budget -- 16 units attempted of a 5,061-unit
union (0.3%). 2,571 candidates carry an OFFLINE OA URL (already discovered, zero network to resolve) and
were NEVER SPENT. So we have a census of a barely-started run, not the access ceiling. Discovery is DONE
and proved the literature is NOT thin.

Census findings you must honour:
 * CEILING IS ACCESS-CONTROL, NOT THROTTLING: zero 429s; 24x 403 (Wiley/Elsevier-JS/MDPI/OUP). So
   PRIORITISE routes that actually return OA full text: PMC / Europe PMC / DOAJ / publisher-OA-PDF /
   OpenAlex best_oa_location pdf_url. DE-PRIORITISE Wiley/Elsevier-direct/MDPI-direct (they 403).
 * THE P0 TRIED TO CRAWL BACK: the reducer scored a GLO Discussion Paper and an arXiv preprint AS
   JOURNAL FULL TEXT. _PREPRINT_STAMP (event_ledger.py:381) knows nber|iza|arxiv|"this version:" but NOT
   "GLO Discussion Paper", "Discussion Paper No.", "Working Paper", "SSRN", "RePEc", "MPRA", "Munich
   Personal RePEc", "Documento de Trabajo", "Cahier de recherche", etc. FIX THIS FIRST.

Candidate lists on disk (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/acquisition_campaign/): frontier_2023_2025_FINAL.json, oa_cell_discovery.json,
canon_and_contrasts_leads.json, citation-graph output, _known_dois.json, campaign_ledger.jsonl,
census_probe.py. Tools: acquisition.py, deep_fetch.py, provenance.py, event_ledger.py, weighting.py.


YOUR TASK: extend the preprint/working-paper header detector so a working paper CANNOT pass as journal
full text. The census caught the reducer scoring a GLO Discussion Paper and an arXiv preprint as journal
FULLTEXT -- the exact P0 failure, crawling back through a gap in the stamp list.

 1. In event_ledger.py (~:381, `_PREPRINT_STAMP`), extend the pattern to recognise, case-insensitively,
    at least: "GLO Discussion Paper", "Discussion Paper No", "Working Paper", "SSRN", "RePEc", "MPRA",
    "Munich Personal RePEc", "IZA DP", "NBER Working Paper", "CEPR Discussion Paper", "Documento de
    Trabajo", "Cahier de recherche", "econstor", "preprint", "This version:", "arXiv:". Header/first-page
    scan. If a stamp is present -> the manifestation is a PREPRINT/WORKING-PAPER expression, NOT
    journal_version, so it is a DISCOVERY LEAD under journal-only policy.
 2. This must be DATA-DRIVEN where possible (a list/registry), not a wall of hardcoded ifs -- adding a new
    repository stamp should be a data edit.
 3. Add/extend a canary or self-test proving: a GLO Discussion Paper header and an arXiv-stamped header are
    classified preprint (NOT journal full text), and a genuine journal header still passes. Then run
    `python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` (must stay green).

Report exactly what you changed (file:line), the stamps you added, and the test output. Do NOT commit.

387	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
388	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
389	
390	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
391	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
392	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
393	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
394	    """
395	    words = text.split()
396	    header = text[:header_chars]
397	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
398	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
399	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
400	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
401	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
402	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
403	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
404	    return {

406	        'char_count': len(text),
407	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
408	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
409	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
410	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(header)),
411	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(header)),
412	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
413	        'header_real_words': header_real,
414	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
415	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
416	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
417	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
418	        'identity_window': header,
419	        'header_text': header[:400],
420	    }

423	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
424	# REDUCERS — the ONLY place a label may come into existence.

429	RESPONDED            = 'RESPONDED'                    # the backend answered with content
430	NOT_INDEXED          = 'NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND'  # 404 — a fact about THEIR INDEX, not the world
431	BACKEND_FAILED       = 'BACKEND_FAILED'               # 429/503/timeout — a fact about OUR REQUEST
432	ACCESS_BLOCKED       = 'ACCESS_BLOCKED'               # 401/403/paywall — a fact about ENTITLEMENT
433	NO_OUTCOME           = 'NO_OUTCOME'                   # attempted, never came back. A HANG, not a gap.
434	
435	
436	#: The four kinds that constitute A REQUEST. CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED and MANIFESTATION_FETCHED also carry
437	#: an `adapter` (for provenance — WHICH backend found this), but they are not request outcomes and must
438	#: not be read as one.
439	_REQUEST_KINDS = (EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED,
440	                  EventKind.THROTTLED, EventKind.BLOCKED)
441	
442	#: Worst-first. An adapter that hung on one request and answered another has NOT answered: we asked it
443	#: something and never found out. Between RESPONDED and NOT_INDEXED, RESPONDED wins — a backend that
444	#: answered "not in my index" for one query and returned a paper for another IS WORKING.
445	_OUTCOME_PRECEDENCE = (NO_OUTCOME, BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED, RESPONDED, NOT_INDEXED)
446	
447	
448	def _outcome_of_request(evs: list[Event]) -> str:
449	    """The outcome of ONE logical request, read from its FINAL terminal event.
450	
451	    A retry loop emits BACKEND_ATTEMPTED + one terminal event PER ATTEMPT, all sharing a `request_id`.
452	    So a request reads:  ATTEMPTED, THROTTLED, ATTEMPTED, THROTTLED, ATTEMPTED, RESPONSE_RECEIVED(200)
453	
454	    The 429s ARE NOT DELETED — they are on disk forever, and `Ledger` has no operation that could
455	    remove them. But a throttle the backoff RESOLVED and a throttle we GAVE UP ON are different facts
456	    about the world, and only the second one means we never got the answer. Reading "any 429 anywhere"
457	    as BACKEND_FAILED would mark every route degraded the moment a single retry succeeded, and a route
458	    that is always degraded can never license a scoped absence — which sounds conservative and is
459	    actually just as blind as always calling it complete.

809	# ---- 4. semantic binding ---------------------------------------------------------------------
810	
811	SAME_WORK        = 'SAME_WORK'              # this IS the journal article
812	VERSION_PUBLISHED = 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED'  # a repository copy OF the published version
813	VERSION_PREPRINT = 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'    # a working paper. A DIFFERENT SOURCE until proven equal.
814	DIFFERENT_WORK   = 'DIFFERENT_WORK'         # we fetched somebody else's paper
815	UNRESOLVED       = 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
816	
817	
818	def _norm(s: str) -> set[str]:
819	    stop = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'of', 'and', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'from', 'with'}
820	    return {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]+', (s or '').lower()) if w not in stop and len(w) > 2}
821	
822	
823	def derive_semantic_binding(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
824	    """Is the text we fetched the work we asked for? A fetcher MAY NOT ANSWER THIS ABOUT ITSELF.
825	
826	    Sol: "A predecessor working paper and later journal article may be closely related, but they are
827	    DIFFERENT SOURCES until version equivalence is proven."
828	    """
829	    # THE SAME DOCUMENT THE LABEL DESCRIBES. `_best_holding` returns a manifestation TOGETHER WITH ITS
830	    # OWN profile — so identity is checked against the header of the very bytes whose completeness was
831	    # judged. Reading "the last manifestation" and "the last profile" independently could pair one
832	    # document's byline with another document's word count, and answer about a document that does not
833	    # exist.
834	    best = _best_holding(events)
835	    if best is None:
836	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': 'nothing was fetched'}
837	    fetched, prof, _cls, _info = best
838	
839	    # The identity window is BOUNDED (see observe_text): a whole-document scan finds every author in
840	    # the references and "confirms" anything you ask it to.
841	    window = fetched.get('document_title', '') or prof.get('identity_window', '') \
842	        or prof.get('header_text', '')
843	    want_t = _norm(fetched.get('requested_title', ''))
844	    got_t = _norm(window)
845	    want_a = {a.lower() for a in fetched.get('requested_authors', [])}
846	    byline = (fetched.get('byline', '') or window).lower()
847	
848	    overlap = len(want_t & got_t) / max(1, len(want_t))
849	    author_hit = any(a in byline for a in want_a if a)
850	    title_match = overlap >= 0.90
851	    # A SPECIFIC title (>=4 content words) matching in full is itself strong identity evidence.
852	    # A GENERIC one is not: {rise, machines} sits entirely inside {mathematics, rise, machines}.
853	    title_specific = len(want_t) >= 4
854	
855	    ev = {'title_overlap': round(overlap, 2), 'author_in_byline': author_hit,
856	          'title_content_words': len(want_t),
857	          'requested_title': fetched.get('requested_title', '')[:70],
858	          'header_text': (prof.get('header_text') or '')[:120]}
859	
860	    # ── AN UNREADABLE HEADER IS NOT A STRANGER'S PAPER. ───────────────────────────────────────
861	    # Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) arrives as a PDF whose title page is pure (cid:NN) glyph codes:
862	    # ZERO readable words in the header, though the BODY has 10,902. An earlier version of this
863	    # reducer read "no title, no author" as DIFFERENT_WORK — turning a FONT-ENCODING FAILURE into a
864	    # confident claim about whose paper it is. That is this project's whole disease, committed by
865	    # the cure. We cannot read it, therefore we cannot bind it. Say exactly that.
866	    if prof.get('header_real_words', 0) < 10:
867	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'the header decoded to {prof.get("header_real_words", 0)} '
868	                                      f'readable words — we CANNOT READ it, so we cannot establish '
869	                                      f'identity. Not a stranger\'s paper: an unreadable one', **ev}
870	
871	    # ── IDENTITY IS CONFIRMED BY AN AUTHOR, OR BY A SPECIFIC TITLE IN FULL. ───────────────────
872	    confirmed = author_hit or (title_match and title_specific)
873	
874	    if not confirmed:
875	        # DIFFERENT_WORK is a STRONG CLAIM ("we are holding a stranger's paper") and it needs
876	        # POSITIVE evidence. Absence of a confirming author is NOT that evidence — it is absence of
877	        # evidence, and reading the two as the same is how "429" became "no free copy exists".
878	        if title_match and not title_specific:
879	            return DIFFERENT_WORK, {
880	                'reason': f'the requested title has only {len(want_t)} content word(s), and no '
881	                          f'requested author appears in the header. A {overlap:.0%} match on a '
882	                          f'GENERIC title is a TITLE COLLISION, not identity — the short title sits '
883	                          f'inside a stranger\'s longer one', **ev}
884	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'title overlap {overlap:.0%}, no requested author in the '
885	                                      f'header — identity is NOT established, so this text may not '
886	                                      f'be attributed to this source', **ev}
887	
888	    # ...an author alone is not identity either: prolific authors write many papers.
889	    if author_hit and not title_match and not title_specific:
890	        return DIFFERENT_WORK, {'reason': f'the author matches but the title does not ({overlap:.0%}) '
891	                                          f'— same author, DIFFERENT PAPER', **ev}
892	
893	    if prof.get('published_stamp_in_header'):
894	        return VERSION_PUBLISHED, {'reason': 'identity confirmed, and the header carries a '
895	                                             'published-version/refereed stamp', **ev}
896	    if prof.get('preprint_stamp_in_header'):
897	        return VERSION_PREPRINT, {'reason': 'identity confirmed, but the header carries a '
898	                                            'preprint/working-paper stamp — peer review CHANGES '
899	                                            'NUMBERS; this is a DIFFERENT SOURCE until version '
900	                                            'equivalence is proven', **ev}
901	    return SAME_WORK, {'reason': 'identity confirmed by '
902	                                 + ('author and title' if author_hit and title_match
903	                                    else 'author in the header' if author_hit
904	                                    else f'a specific {len(want_t)}-word title matched in full')
905	                                 + ', and no preprint stamp in the header', **ev}
906	
907	
908	# ---- 5. eligibility --------------------------------------------------------------------------
909	
910	ADMISSIBLE     = 'ADMISSIBLE'
911	DISCOVERY_LEAD = 'DISCOVERY_LEAD'     # Sol's word, exactly. Real text — but not attributable HERE.
912	INADMISSIBLE   = 'INADMISSIBLE'
913	
914	
915	def derive_eligibility(events: list[Event], journal_articles_only: bool = True) -> tuple[str, dict]:
916	    """May this text be attributed to THIS source, under THIS task's instruction?
917	
918	    Task 72 demands JOURNAL ARTICLES ONLY. So citing the working paper BREAKS THE INSTRUCTION, and
919	    citing the journal with the working paper's text IS FABRICATION. There is no version in which the
920	    working-paper span ships as journal evidence.
921	    """
922	    cls, cinfo = derive_content_profile(events)
923	    binding, binfo = derive_semantic_binding(events)
924	
925	    if cls == C_NOT_DOC:
926	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'not a document ({cinfo["reason"]})',
927	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
928	    if cls == C_UNREADABLE:
929	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'unreadable ({cinfo["reason"]})',
930	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
931	    if binding == DIFFERENT_WORK:
932	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'wrong paper ({binfo["reason"]})',
933	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
934	    if binding == VERSION_PREPRINT:
935	        return DISCOVERY_LEAD, {
936	            'reason': 'working-paper text. A DISCOVERY LEAD, not automatically journal-attributable '
937	                      'evidence — version equivalence is not proven'
938	                      + (' and the task demands journal articles only' if journal_articles_only else ''),
939	            'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
940	    if binding == UNRESOLVED:
941	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'we cannot show this text is the work we cite',
942	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
943	    if cls == C_CITATION:
944	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'no content', 'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Throwaway probe: measure the false-positive blast radius of the widened _PREPRINT_STAMP
registry against the REAL corpus BEFORE editing event_ledger.py. We must not turn a genuine
journal article (identity confirmed, currently ADMISSIBLE) into a DISCOVERY_LEAD.

Reads the corpus read-only. Writes nothing.
"""
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path('/home/polaris/wt/flywheel')
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))

import event_ledger as EL

CORPUS = json.loads((ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json').read_text())

# The candidate registry -> one compiled alternation (mirrors the planned edit).
CANDIDATE_STAMPS = [
    ('nber',              r'nber working paper|national bureau of economic research'),
    ('iza',               r'iza (?:dp|discussion paper)'),
    ('glo',               r'glo discussion paper'),
    ('cepr',              r'cepr discussion paper'),
    ('generic-wp',        r'working paper'),
    ('generic-dp',        r'discussion paper no'),
    ('ssrn',              r'\bssrn\b'),
    ('repec',             r'\brepec\b'),
    ('mpra',              r'\bmpra\b'),
    ('munich-repec',      r'munich personal repec'),
    ('econstor',          r'econstor'),
    ('doc-trabajo',       r'documento de trabajo'),
    ('cahier',            r'cahier de recherche'),
    ('arxiv',             r'arxiv:\s*\d'),
    ('preprint',          r'\bpreprint\b'),
    ('this-version',      r'this (?:draft|version):'),
    ('preliminary',       r'preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|do not (?:cite|quote) without'),
]
NEW = re.compile('|'.join(f'(?:{p})' for _, p in CANDIDATE_STAMPS), re.I)
OLD = EL._PREPRINT_STAMP


def which(header):
    return sorted({name for name, p in CANDIDATE_STAMPS if re.search(p, header, re.I)})


def elig_for(row, stamp_re):
    """Re-derive eligibility for one FULLTEXT row using a chosen preprint regex."""
    orig = EL._PREPRINT_STAMP
    EL._PREPRINT_STAMP = stamp_re
    try:
        L = EL.Ledger()
        u = row.get('doi') or row.get('title', '?')
        L.emit(u, EL.EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'corpus', url=row.get('oa_url', ''),
               requested_title=row.get('title', ''), requested_authors=row.get('authors', []))
        L.emit(u, EL.EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observer',
               **EL.observe_text(row.get('fulltext') or ''))
        b, _ = EL.derive_semantic_binding(L.events(u))
        e, _ = EL.derive_eligibility(L.events(u))
        return b, e
    finally:
        EL._PREPRINT_STAMP = orig


flips_bad = []   # ADMISSIBLE -> DISCOVERY_LEAD  (false positive we must not cause)
flips_good = []  # anything -> DISCOVERY_LEAD that was NOT a lead before (real catches)
for r in CORPUS:
    if r.get('content_status') != 'FULLTEXT':
        continue
    key = f"{(r.get('authors') or ['?'])[0]} {r.get('year')}"
    b_old, e_old = elig_for(r, OLD)
    b_new, e_new = elig_for(r, NEW)
    if e_old != e_new:
        header = (r.get('fulltext') or '')[:1500]
        entry = (key, r.get('title', '')[:60], f'{b_old}->{b_new}', f'{e_old}->{e_new}', which(header))
        if e_old == EL.ADMISSIBLE and e_new == EL.DISCOVERY_LEAD:
            flips_bad.append(entry)
        else:
            flips_good.append(entry)

print(f'FULLTEXT rows: {sum(1 for r in CORPUS if r.get("content_status")=="FULLTEXT")}')
print(f'\n### BAD FLIPS (genuine journal article demoted to discovery lead) : {len(flips_bad)}')
for k, t, bb, ee, w in flips_bad:
    print(f'  !! {k:<20} {ee:<28} stamps={w}\n     title: {t}')
print(f'\n### OTHER FLIPS (newly caught as lead / other) : {len(flips_good)}')
for k, t, bb, ee, w in flips_good:
    print(f'  -> {k:<20} {ee:<28} stamps={w}\n     title: {t}')

# Also: for every currently-ADMISSIBLE row, does the header contain ANY new stamp?
print('\n### ADMISSIBLE rows whose header trips ANY candidate stamp (audit):')
any_trip = 0
for r in CORPUS:
    if r.get('content_status') != 'FULLTEXT':
        continue
    b_old, e_old = elig_for(r, OLD)
    if e_old != EL.ADMISSIBLE:
        continue
    header = (r.get('fulltext') or '')[:1500]
    w = which(header)
    if w:
        any_trip += 1
        key = f"{(r.get('authors') or ['?'])[0]} {r.get('year')}"
        print(f'  {key:<20} stamps={w}')
        # show the matching context so we can judge if it is a real stamp or a false hit
        for name, p in CANDIDATE_STAMPS:
            m = re.search(p, header, re.I)
            if m:
                s = max(0, m.start() - 40)
                print(f'      [{name}] ...{header[s:m.end()+40]!r}')
if not any_trip:
    print('  (none)')

name: architecture-conformance-required

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review]
    paths:
      - 'src/polaris_graph/**'
      - 'scripts/dr_benchmark/**'
      - 'scripts/architecture/**'

jobs:
  architecture-conformance:
    name: architecture-conformance-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Compute PR issue id from branch
        id: issue
        run: |
          BRANCH="${{ github.head_ref }}"
          # Expect bot/I-<prefix>-<NNN>(-...) — extract I-<prefix>-<NNN>
          ISSUE_ID=$(echo "$BRANCH" | sed -nE 's|bot/(I-[a-z0-9]+-[0-9]+[a-z]?).*|\1|p')
          if [ -z "$ISSUE_ID" ]; then
            echo "::error::cannot derive issue id from branch '$BRANCH'"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "issue_id=$ISSUE_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Issue id: $ISSUE_ID"

      - name: Check architecture conformance attestation exists
        run: |
          ISSUE_ID="${{ steps.issue.outputs.issue_id }}"
          ATTESTATION=".codex/${ISSUE_ID}/architecture_conformance_attestation.md"
          if [ ! -f "$ATTESTATION" ]; then
            echo "::error::missing required attestation: $ATTESTATION"
            echo "::error::Copy from .codex/architecture_conformance_attestation_template.md and fill in every section."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Attestation found: $ATTESTATION"

      - name: Validate attestation YAML footer
        run: |
          ISSUE_ID="${{ steps.issue.outputs.issue_id }}"
          ATTESTATION=".codex/${ISSUE_ID}/architecture_conformance_attestation.md"
          python <<'PY'
          import os, re, sys, yaml
          path = os.environ.get("ATTESTATION", ".codex/${ISSUE_ID}/architecture_conformance_attestation.md")
          import os
          issue = os.environ.get("ISSUE_ID")
          path = f".codex/{issue}/architecture_conformance_attestation.md"
          text = open(path, encoding="utf-8").read()
          m = re.search(r"```yaml\s*(.*?)```", text, re.DOTALL)

name: cleanup_pr_ancestry_check
# Per state/polaris_restart/cleanup_audit.md (Codex APPROVE iter 21) lines
# 952-1006 + iter-18/19/20 fixes CLEAN-DEPS-ANCESTRY-STILL-INCOMPLETE-18
# and CLEAN-ANCESTRY-SHALLOW-CHECKOUT-19.
#
# Verifies Cleanup-PR-N branches merge in linear DAG order:
# 1 -> 2 -> 3a -> 3b -> 3c -> 4 -> 5 -> 6 -> 7 -> 8.
#
# Reads the predecessor PR's merge-commit SHA from
# state/polaris_restart/cleanup_pr_dependencies.json and uses
# `git merge-base --is-ancestor` to confirm the predecessor is in the
# current PR's ancestry.
#
# Hardening note (PR-D iter 2 PRD2-P1-003 fix): PR-controlled head ref
# passed via `env:` and validated against a narrow allowlist regex BEFORE
# being used in bash. Never interpolated as `${{ ... }}` directly into
# shell source.

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [polaris]
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  verify_predecessor_merged:
    if: startsWith(github.event.pull_request.head.ref, 'cleanup/pr-')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      HEAD_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          # iter 20 CLEAN-ANCESTRY-SHALLOW-CHECKOUT-19 fix: full history
          # needed for `git merge-base --is-ancestor` to find predecessor
          # merge SHAs. Default checkout depth is 1.
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: extract_pr_id
        id: pr
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # PR-D iter 2 PRD2-P1-003 fix: read $HEAD_REF from env (not from
          # `${{ ... }}` interpolated into shell source). Validate against
          # narrow allowlist of canonical 10-PR ids.
          # Branch convention: `cleanup/pr-<id>[-<slug>]` where <id> is one
          # of 1, 2, 3a, 3b, 3c, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8.
          # Bash regex with extended quoting; rejects any branch whose id
          # token is not in the allowlist (e.g. `cleanup/pr-2evil` does
          # not match because `2evil` is not on the list).
          if [[ "$HEAD_REF" =~ ^cleanup/pr-(1|2|3a|3b|3c|4|5|6|7|8)(-[A-Za-z0-9_-]+)?$ ]]; then
            pr_id="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
            printf 'id=%s\n' "$pr_id" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "Validated cleanup-PR head ref: cleanup/pr-$pr_id"
          else
            echo "ERROR: head ref '$HEAD_REF' does not match cleanup/pr-<canonical_id>[-<slug>]"
            echo "       allowed ids: 1, 2, 3a, 3b, 3c, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8"
            exit 1

# - Verdict files must declare a final verdict block at the END of the
#   artifact (PRD2-P1-001) so transcript prompt text containing the literal
#   string "verdict: APPROVE" cannot fool the gate. The convention is:
#   the LAST `verdict: APPROVE` or `verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES` line in the
#   file is treated as authoritative.

# mounted `~/.codex/auth.json` so `codex exec` runs ON THE RUNNER per
# §10.0 line 898. Until then, this workflow parses verdict files committed
# by Claude; user reads `git log` morning per §7.B B1 as after-the-fact

concurrency:

jobs:

name: codex-verdict-check

# Plan v13 §C three-phase gate. Server-side cryptographic re-validation
# of the local Codex verdict via independently-run Codex on a self-hosted
# Canadian runner (sovereignty per §C-server P1-15).
#
# Phase A (trusted-setup): checkout PR + extract task brief into artifact.
#   No model keys exposed.
# Phase B (codex-rerun): in Docker egress jail with Caddy reverse-proxy
#   sidecar. Real OPENAI_API_KEY lives in proxy container env only;
#   Codex container has dummy "REDACTED-USE-PROXY" + OPENAI_BASE_URL.
#   No GitHub creds. Only network egress: api.openai.com:443 via proxy.
# Phase C (verdict-validate): validator code from trusted main ref
#   (PR cannot tamper). Only CODEX_HMAC_KEY in env. Validates schema +
#   HMAC + canonical pin + structural-equivalence per Plan v13 §C
#   severity-aware rule.

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [polaris]
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read

concurrency:
  group: codex-verdict-${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:

  trusted-setup:
    name: Phase A — trusted setup (no model keys)
    runs-on: [self-hosted, polaris-ca-bhs]
    timeout-minutes: 5
    outputs:
      task_id: ${{ steps.extract.outputs.task_id }}
      diff_sha256: ${{ steps.extract.outputs.diff_sha256 }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Extract task_id from commit message
        id: extract
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Look across ALL commits in the PR, not just last (Codex P1-7 + P0-7 fix)
          MSG=$(git log "origin/polaris..HEAD" --format=%B)
          TASK_ID=$(echo "$MSG" | grep -oP "^task:\s*\K[\w.]+" | head -1 || true)
          if [[ -z "$TASK_ID" ]]; then
              echo "::error::PR commit message(s) missing required `task: <task_id>` line per Plan v13 §I."
              exit 1
          fi
          echo "task_id=$TASK_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

name: legacy-protection

# CI gate enforcing the .legacy/ contract per .legacy/README.md:
#   1. No active production code may import from .legacy/*
#   2. No PR may modify any existing file under .legacy/
#   (3. Adding new files to .legacy/ via git mv is allowed)
#
# Runs on every PR targeting any branch. Fails the PR (commit-status: failure)
# on violation, blocking merge per branch protection's required-status-checks.

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: ['*']

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read

jobs:
  legacy_protection:
    name: legacy-protection
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR head
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}

      - name: Compute base..head diff
        id: diff
        run: |
          BASE_SHA="${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}"
          HEAD_SHA="${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
          echo "base=$BASE_SHA" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "head=$HEAD_SHA" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Files modified or deleted in .legacy/ (additions are OK)
          git diff --name-status "$BASE_SHA" "$HEAD_SHA" -- '.legacy/' \
            | awk '$1 != "A"' > /tmp/legacy_modifications.txt || true
          # All changed files (for import scanning)
          git diff --name-only "$BASE_SHA" "$HEAD_SHA" > /tmp/all_changed_files.txt || true

      - name: Reject modifications to existing .legacy/ files
        run: |
          if [ -s /tmp/legacy_modifications.txt ]; then
            echo "::error title=Legacy modification detected::Files under .legacy/ are read-only post-add per .legacy/README.md."
            echo ""
            echo "Modified or deleted files in .legacy/:"
            cat /tmp/legacy_modifications.txt
            echo ""
            echo "Resolution: revert these changes. .legacy/ is a read-only archive."
            echo "If you need to update old code, re-write under the active slice."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "OK: no modifications to existing .legacy/ files."

      - name: Reject new imports from .legacy/
        run: |
          # Scan files newly added or modified outside .legacy/ for imports
          # referencing .legacy/ paths.

name: status-schema-parity-required

# LANE-CI (B23) — I-arch-005 Phase-2/3 (umbrella #1257).
#
# Asserts STATUS-SCHEMA PARITY: every terminal status that
# scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py can write into manifest.status
# (UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES + the to_unified_status / _SUMMARY_TO_UNIFIED map) is
# present in the shared downstream registries that consume that field
# (PipelineStatus in src/polaris_v6/schemas/run_status.py and KNOWN_STATUS_VALUES
# in src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/regression_lab.py). A runner status missing
# downstream = CI FAIL (it 500s RunStatusResponse on a real run).
#
# Read-only STATIC scan: AST-parses all three source files (the runner producer
# set + both downstream registries) and imports NOTHING from src/ or scripts/.
# No pipeline run, no LLM/network, no secrets, no production import chain — so the
# gate cannot ImportError under a minimal CI dependency set.

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review]
    paths:
      # The producer of manifest.status.
      - 'scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py'
      # The downstream consumers (registries) this gate checks parity against.
      - 'src/polaris_v6/schemas/run_status.py'
      - 'src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/regression_lab.py'
      # The gate itself.
      - 'tests/architecture/test_status_schema_parity_b23.py'
      - '.github/workflows/status-schema-parity.yml'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  status-schema-parity:
    name: status-schema-parity-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'

      - name: Install minimal deps
        # The gate AST-parses all three source files and imports nothing from
        # src/ or scripts/, so it needs only the stdlib (ast) + pytest.
        run: python -m pip install -U pytest

      - name: Run status-schema parity gate (static scan, no pipeline run)
        env:
          PYTHONIOENCODING: utf-8
        run: |
          python -m pytest tests/architecture/test_status_schema_parity_b23.py \

#: PREPRINT / WORKING-PAPER STAMPS — a DATA-DRIVEN REGISTRY, not a wall of ifs. Each row is
#: (repository/series key, header-regex fragment). ADDING A NEW REPOSITORY IS A ONE-LINE DATA EDIT.
#:
#: A stamp in a document's OWN header is evidence the manifestation is a WORKING-PAPER / PREPRINT
#: EXPRESSION — NOT the journal version of record. Peer review CHANGES NUMBERS (Acemoglu & Restrepo's
#: robot effect: 0.37pp in the NBER working paper, 0.2pp in the published JPE), so under a journal-only
#: policy a stamped manifestation is a DISCOVERY LEAD, never journal evidence (derive_semantic_binding
#: -> VERSION_PREPRINT -> derive_eligibility -> DISCOVERY_LEAD).
#:
#: WHY THE LIST GREW: the census caught the reducer scoring a GLO Discussion Paper and an arXiv preprint
#: AS JOURNAL FULLTEXT — the P0 crawling back through a gap. The old pattern knew nber|iza|arxiv but NOT
#: the working-paper vocabulary below, so an EconStor working paper (Gray 2013) passed as the journal
#: article. Whenever a new repository stamps its cover page, ADD A ROW HERE — do not touch a reducer.
PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
    #  registry key           header pattern (case-insensitive; matched as a fragment)
    ('nber',              r'nber working paper|national bureau of economic research'),
    ('iza',               r'iza\s+(?:dp|discussion paper)'),
    ('glo',               r'glo discussion paper'),
    ('cepr',              r'cepr discussion paper'),
    ('ssrn',              r'\bssrn\b'),
    ('repec',             r'\brepec\b'),
    ('mpra',              r'\bmpra\b'),
    ('munich_repec',      r'munich personal repec'),
    ('econstor',          r'\beconstor\b'),
    ('documento_trabajo', r'documento de trabajo'),
    ('cahier_recherche',  r'cahier de recherche'),
    ('discussion_paper',  r'discussion paper no'),
    ('working_paper',     r'working paper'),
    ('arxiv',             r'arxiv:\s*\d'),
    ('preprint',          r'\bpreprint\b'),
    ('this_version',      r'this\s+(?:draft|version):'),
    ('preliminary',       r'preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|do not (?:cite|quote) without'),
]

#: The registry compiled to ONE alternation. Named groups let a self-test report WHICH stamp fired
#: (see preprint_stamp_key). observe_text scans the HEADER ONLY: every economics paper CITES NBER
#: working papers in its REFERENCES, and a whole-text scan reads the bibliography, not the paper —
#: which is exactly how a naive scan once called the actual JEP article a working paper.
_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
    '|'.join(f'(?P<{key}>{pat})' for key, pat in PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY), re.I)


def preprint_stamp_key(text: str) -> str | None:
    """The registry key of the FIRST preprint/working-paper stamp in `text`, or None.

    A pure OBSERVATION helper — it CONCLUDES NOTHING. derive_semantic_binding() decides what a stamp
    MEANS (a DIFFERENT SOURCE until version equivalence is proven); this only reports which one is
    present, for provenance and for the self-test that proves the registry covers each repository.
    """
    m = _PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)
    return m.lastgroup if m else None
